etymology.description: semantic 木(木) (mù, "tree") + phonetic 昨(昨) (zuó, "yesterday") -> oak
etymology.interpretation: This character combines the semantic 木(木) (mù, "tree"), indicating that it is a type of tree, with the phonetic component 昨(昨) (zuó), which suggests the pronunciation. The core meaning of "oak" likely stems from its reference as a specific type of tree.

examples.chinese: 这里的森林里有很多柞树(這裡的森林裡有很多柞樹)。
examples.pinyin: Zhèlǐ de sēnlín lǐ yǒu hěn duō zhà shù.
examples.english: There are many oak trees in the forest here.

examples.chinese: 这张桌子是用柞木做的(這張桌子是用柞木做的)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zhāng zhuōzi shì yòng zhà mù zuò de.
examples.english: This table is made of oak wood.

examples.chinese: 在秋天时(在秋天時)，柞树的叶子变成金黄色(柞樹的葉子變成金黃色)。
examples.pinyin: Zài qiūtiān shí, zhà shù de yèzi biàn chéng jīnhuángsè.
examples.english: In autumn, the leaves of the oak tree turn golden yellow.
